heroHeadline: Day 28: CJNG Succession Vacuum Deepens; Carteles Unidos Advances in Michoacán
heroSubtitle: Day 28 since El Mencho's killing at Tapalpa. El Menchito (Rubén Oseguera González), El Mencho's designated successor, remains imprisoned in the United States with no clear heir emerging. Brookings Institution expert Vanda Felbab-Brown warns violence will continue 'in a different pattern, in a different form, and with different motivation' as the succession crisis extends into its fourth week. Rival criminal organization Carteles Unidos is moving to exploit the power vacuum in Michoacán, with armed confrontations reported across multiple municipalities as factions contest territory previously held by CJNG. Security experts warn of prolonged violent realignments lasting months to potentially years, driven by the absence of unified command across CJNG's sprawling 27-state presence.
footerNote: Data sourced from SEDENA, SSPC, FGR, DEA, U.S. DOJ, InSight Crime, Crisis Group, Reuters, AP, Excélsior, Infobae México, La Silla Rota, Janes, and major Mexican news outlets. All contested figures noted. Last update: March 22, 2026.
